title: Synthesia is no longer just about videos: It wants AI to immediately retort you and then rate you
excerpt: For years, Synthesia's signature has been using AI to create interactive training videos for businesses in minutes, at a fraction of the cost of traditional cor
content_html:
For years, Synthesia's signature has been using AI to create interactive training videos for businesses in minutes, at a fraction of the cost of traditional corporate learning materials. Now, this UK startup is betting on another angle: perhaps the real moat for enterprise AI lies not in content generation, but in proving "it actually works."

On Wednesday, Synthesia launched Roleplay Sessions, an interactive training product. Employees can practice high-stakes conversations—such as sales pitches, performance reviews, and customer complaints—with an AI avatar that talks back, argues, and finally gives you a score based on a rating scale. This is the first product under Synthesia's broader "Sessions" platform, which, according to exclusive information from TechCrunch, will expand to other forms such as interviews and candidate screening.

This move comes at a time when companies are re-evaluating whether AI spending is worth it. Synthesia cites a meta-analysis of learning studies showing that most corporate training—including its core video product—often stops at "changing behavior": they excel at informing and demonstrating, but the real push into learning comes from doing it yourself, plus feedback. "Videos are way better than just text or sending a document," said CEO and co-founder Viktor Heston to TechCrunch, "but for most things, our best way to learn isn't reading—it's actually practicing."

Roleplay also positions Synthesia more securely. The company's avatars and voice technology are self-developed, but the underlying reasoning intelligence comes from OpenAI. Once layered with rating scales, performance data, and analytics, Synthesia becomes more like a performance management platform with a video facade, rather than just an AI avatar company. "From a leadership perspective, we see strong interest—before it was hard to understand the distribution of talent in the company, now it's possible," said Heston, "if you have your entire sales team practice with AI characters, you can get very granular data on how the team performs overall."

Synthesia already has several early Roleplay clients who have scaled commercial deployments, including "one of the top three most valuable companies in Europe, one of the top five in the Fortune 100, and one of the largest recruitment companies in the world." The two most popular scenarios right now are sales teams and leadership training—such as how to sell a product or handle a difficult conversation, Heston said, with a lot being soft skills training. Like its main product, customers can build their own training programs on the platform, or ask Synthesia's consultants to set up a customized solution, partly relying on the company's existing training documents and background.

Roleplay is currently an enterprise product, but the goal is to make it more widely available to small businesses, professionals, and possibly educational institutions in the coming months, as reasoning costs continue to drop. Heston believes that products like Roleplay represent the next phase of enterprise AI implementation: companies are no longer so concerned about how AI looks in social media demos, but rather whether it can produce measurable business results.

sentences.en: Roleplay is currently an enterprise product, but the goal is to make it more widely available to small businesses, professionals, and possibly educational institutions in the coming months, as reasoning costs continue to drop.
sentences.zh: 角色扮演目前是一种企业产品，但随着推理成本持续下降，我们的目标是在未来几个月内将其更广泛地提供给小型企业、专业人士，甚至可能包括教育机构。

sentences.en: Heston believes that products like Roleplay represent the next phase of enterprise AI implementation: companies are no longer so concerned about how AI looks in social media demos, but rather whether it can produce measurable business results.
sentences.zh: Heston 认为，像 Roleplay 这样的产品代表了企业人工智能实施的下一阶段：企业不再那么关心人工智能在社交媒体演示中的表现，而是它是否能够产生可衡量的业务成果。

content_html_zh:
多年来，Synthesia 的招牌一直是使用人工智能在几分钟内为企业创建交互式培训视频，而成本只是传统企业学习材料的一小部分。 现在，这家英国初创公司正押注于另一个角度：也许企业人工智能的真正护城河不在于内容生成，而在于证明“它确实有效”。

周三，Synthesia 推出了互动培训产品 Roleplay Sessions。 员工可以通过人工智能化身练习高风险对话，例如销售推介、绩效评估和客户投诉，该化身会进行顶嘴、争论，并最终根据评分标准给你一个分数。 这是Synthesia更广泛的“Sessions”平台下的第一个产品，根据TechCrunch的独家信息，该平台将扩展到面试和候选人筛选等其他形式。

此举正值公司重新评估人工智能支出是否值得之际。 Synthesia 引用了一项学习研究的荟萃分析，该分析表明，大多数企业培训（包括其核心视频产品）往往停留在“改变行为”：他们擅长告知和演示，但真正推动学习的动力来自于自己动手以及反馈。 首席执行官兼联合创始人维克托·赫斯顿 (Viktor Heston) 向 TechCrunch 表示：“视频比单纯发短信或发送文档要好得多，但对于大多数事情来说，我们最好的学习方式不是阅读，而是实际练习。”

Roleplay 也使 Synthesia 的地位更加稳固。 该公司的虚拟形象和语音技术是自主研发的，但底层推理智能来自OpenAI。 一旦将评级量表、绩效数据和分析进行分层，Synthesia 就变得更像是一个带有视频外观的绩效管理平台，而不仅仅是一家人工智能化身公司。 “从领导层的角度来看，我们看到了强烈的兴趣——以前很难了解公司的人才分布，现在这是可能的，”赫斯顿说，“如果你让整个销售团队都用人工智能角色进行练习，你就可以获得有关团队整体表现的非常详细的数据。”

Synthesia 已经拥有多个已扩大商业部署规模的早期 Roleplay 客户，其中包括“欧洲最具价值的三大公司之一、财富 100 强前五名的公司之一以及世界上最大的招聘公司之一。” 赫斯顿说，目前最流行的两个场景是销售团队和领导力培训，例如如何销售产品或处理困难的对话，其中很多是软技能培训。 与其主要产品一样，客户可以在该平台上构建自己的培训计划，或者要求 Synthesia 的顾问建立定制的解决方案，部分依赖该公司现有的培训文档和背景。

角色扮演目前是一种企业产品，但随着推理成本持续下降，我们的目标是在未来几个月内将其更广泛地提供给小型企业、专业人士，甚至可能包括教育机构。 Heston 认为，像 Roleplay 这样的产品代表了企业人工智能实施的下一阶段：企业不再那么关心人工智能在社交媒体演示中的表现，而是它是否能够产生可衡量的业务成果。
